---
title: "Floating Marsh Pennywort (Hydrocotyle ranunculoides)"
slug: "hydrocotyle-ranunculoides"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474863598/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Floating Marsh Pennywort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Apiales"
tax_family: "Araliaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Apiales", "Araliaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474863598/medium.jpg" alt="Floating Marsh Pennywort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/elion592">
elion592</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264433684">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Apiales
-   **Family:** Araliaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=60951)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats.

## Natural history notes

A creeping aquatic plant characterized by its round, scalloped leaves borne on long stems that float on the water’s surface. In Florida, it is commonly found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats such as ponds, ditches, and marshes. It grows during the warmer months and can spread rapidly, forming dense mats that provide cover for aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/60951-Hydrocotyle-ranunculoides)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/hydrocotyle-species/)
